name: APPLIED MATERIALS INC /DE
business: Applied Materials, Inc. is the leader in the materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every semiconductor in the world. The company designs, develops, manufactures and sells a wide range of equipment used to fabricate semiconductor chips, as well as services, spares and factory automation software. It also manufactures equipment for the display industry.
sellsTo: Foundry, logic and other semiconductor markets
End markets including personal computing devices, mobile phones, AI and data center servers, automobiles, connected devices, industrial applications, and consumer electronics

exposures.sentence: The growth of data and emerging end-market drivers such as artificial intelligence, the internet of things, robotics and smart vehicles are also creating the next wave of growth for the industry.

Our customers products are used across personal computing devices, mobile phones, artificial intelligence (AI) and data center servers, automobiles, connected devices, industrial applications and consumer electronics.

Over the longer term, we believe secular drivers such as data center AI, edge AI and the internet of things, robotics and electric and autonomous vehicles will continue to create the next wave of growth for semiconductors and expand our served market opportunities.
Applied Materials, Inc. is the leader in the materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every semiconductor in the world.
Our Semiconductor Systems segment designs, develops, manufactures and sells a wide range of equipment used to fabricate semiconductor chips.

Semiconductors provide the foundation for advances in technology that are reshaping the global economy, including artificial intelligence, the internet of things, robotics, electric and autonomous vehicles, and clean energy.

For example, in 2025 the Chinese government implemented export controls on the export of rare earth minerals that are used in certain of our products and may implement additional controls in the future.
We and our third-party providers have experienced, and expect to continue to experience, cybersecurity incidents.
Advances in quantum computing have the potential to undermine current encryption standards and may allow threat actors to circumvent existing protective measures.

catalysts.event: 200mm equipment business moved to Semiconductor Systems segment
catalysts.sentence: Effective the first quarter of fiscal 2026, our 200mm equipment business will be moved to our Semiconductor Systems segment.

catalysts.event: Workforce reduction plan approved (Fiscal 2025 Restructuring Plan)
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, we approved a workforce reduction plan (Fiscal 2025 Restructuring Plan) to position us for continued growth as a more competitive and productive organization and expect approximately 4% of our global workforce to be impacted under this plan.
We expect to complete the plan in fiscal 2026.

catalysts.event: Issuance of $550M 4.000% notes due 2031 and $450M 4.600% notes due 2036
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we issued $550 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.000% senior unsecured notes due 2031 and $450 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.600% senior unsecured notes due 2036, in a registered public offering.

catalysts.event: Repayment of $700M 3.900% senior notes due October 1, 2025
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we used a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to repay the outstanding $700 million in aggregate principal amount of our 3.900% senior unsecured notes due October 1, 2025.

catalysts.event: Conditional reduced tax rates in Singapore expire
catalysts.sentence: We have been granted additional conditional reduced tax rates in Singapore that expire beginning in fiscal 2030.
